original_text:
### Modular Assembly Conventions **[GENERAL — Solstice production rules]**
- Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding and declared background color; **no margins outside the section** — assembly is direct stacking.
- Section metadata must declare: background color, whether the section establishes `dermatomyositis (DM)`, footnote symbols consumed (†, ‡ assigned at assembly to avoid collisions), and whether it contains a CTA.
- Footnote symbols: superscript, assigned in order of appearance at **assembly time** — sections use placeholder tokens (e.g., `{{fn1}}`) rather than hard-coded daggers when more than one referenced claim exists.
- Locked components (header, footer, ISI) are inserted by the core team; sections adjacent to them must not include decorative elements that visually depend on what's above/below (e.g., a wave that's meant to continue into the footer).
- Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections (assembler responsibility, enabled by declared backgrounds).
- All colors in code as HEX; all sizing in px; inline CSS only (email client compatibility); `bgcolor` attributes duplicated for Outlook.

relations.note: Exclusivity checks for dark background adjacency depend on background properties declared in section metadata.

quotes: self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding
Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding and declared background color; no margins outside the section — assembly is direct stacking.

rules.intent: Maintain email client compatibility and consistent vertical rhythm in stacked modular templates.
rules.rule_text: Each section is a self-contained 600px block (dimension.canvas.width = 600px) with its own top/bottom 32px padding (padding.section.vertical = 32px) and no margins outside the section to enable direct stack assembly.

quotes: Section metadata must declare: background color, whether the section establishes `dermatomyositis (DM)`, footnote symbols consumed (†, ‡ assigned at assembly to avoid collisions), and whether it contains a CTA.

rules.intent: Ensure automated programmatic assemblers have the metadata keys needed to prevent collision and formatting errors.
rules.rule_text: Section metadata must declare: background color, whether the section establishes dermatomyositis (DM), footnote symbols consumed, and whether it contains a CTA.

quotes: Footnote symbols: superscript, assigned in order of appearance at assembly time — sections use placeholder tokens (e.g., `{{fn1}}`) rather than hard-coded daggers when more than one referenced claim exists.

rules.intent: Avoid claim-footnote misalignment and collisions during automated content stacking.
rules.rule_text: Footnote symbols must be superscripts assigned in order of appearance at assembly time. Individual sections must use placeholder tokens (e.g. {{fn1}}) instead of hard-coded daggers when multiple referenced claims exist.

quotes: Locked components (header, footer, ISI) are inserted by the core team; sections adjacent to them must not include decorative elements that visually depend on what's above/below (e.g., a wave that's meant to continue into the footer).

rules.intent: Maintain visual design integrity across dynamically assembled emails.
rules.rule_text: Sections adjacent to locked core-team components (header, footer, ISI) must not include decorative elements that visually depend on what is above or below (such as continuing wave graphics).

quotes: Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections (assembler responsibility, enabled by declared backgrounds).

rules.intent: Maintain typographic contrast boundaries and aesthetic balance across sections.
rules.rule_text: Dark-background sections must not be placed directly adjacent to other dark-background sections during email assembly.

quotes: All colors in code as HEX; all sizing in px; inline CSS only (email client compatibility); `bgcolor` attributes duplicated for Outlook.

rules.intent: Maintain bulletproof compatibility across legacy and modern email clients.
rules.rule_text: All colors in code must be expressed as HEX values; all sizing must be in px; styling must use inline CSS only, with bgcolor attributes duplicated on structural elements for Outlook compatibility.
